query: You are working on the thunderbird/thunderbird-android repository. The codebase is at version 0.1 (commit: a2c9b0ac). ## Issue to Resolve The recipient address is not a valid RFC-5321 address. ### Checklist - [X] I have used the search function to see if someone else has already submitted the same bug report. - [X] I will describe the problem with as much detail as possible. ### App version v6.602 ### Where did you get the app from? Google Play ### Android version 13 ### Device model Moto X40 ### Steps to reproduce Edit a mail, input the recipient address end with a `/`, then send it. https://github.com/thundernest/k-9/assets/10363352/cbb7a99a-b348-4f88-adc3-3fccc1f4ba29 ### Expected behavior We can check the mail address before sending it, toast a message to tell the user that the address is invalid, or show a dialog like Gmail did below: ### Actual behavior Sending it, and showing the failure. ### Logs _No response_ ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `legacy/ui/legacy/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/view/UserInputEmailAddressParser.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.
